title: Ebenus lanceolata
intro: Ebenus lanceolata, also known as the lance-leaved ebony or lance-leaved prunus, is a flowering plant species in the family Rosaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and it is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in relieving digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Ebenus lanceolata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ebenus lanceolata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to skin conditions.
taxonomy: Ebenus lanceolata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Ebenus. The plant is native to parts of the Iberian Peninsula. Ebenus lanceolata is a woody perennial that produces pink flowers. It is typically found in dry, rocky areas and scrublands.
